title: rsa::function and mldsa::sign use 'as u32' truncation without overflow check
description: rsa.rs:63 passes 'in_buf.len() as u32' and mldsa.rs:49 passes 'msg.len() as u32' without TryFrom. On 64-bit systems if in_buf/msg > 4 GiB this truncates silently. The C shim catches it (checks > 0xFFFF), but the inconsistency with every other method in the codebase (which uses u32::try_from) means future callers copying the pattern in a shim without the C guard will silently corrupt the length. Fix: use u32::try_from(...).map_err(...) as done in sign_digest, sha256, etc.
